title: $10,000 in Johnson & Johnson with DRIP (2004)
description: What $10,000 invested in Johnson & Johnson on January 2, 2004 would be worth today with all dividends reinvested. JNJ is a Dividend King — it has raised its dividend every year for over 60 consecutive years. This scenario illustrates the compounding power of DRIP in a blue-chip dividend stock.
dividend_note: JNJ has raised its dividend every year for 62+ consecutive years. Annual dividend per share grew from $1.10 in 2004 to ~$4.76 in 2024. Each reinvestment purchased fractional new shares.
educational_note: DRIP added approximately $23,000 in additional value vs. simply holding without reinvesting dividends. The yield-on-cost (dividend income ÷ original cost basis) reached ~9.2% by 2024 — meaning the original investment now generates ~$920/year in dividends alone.
